2606.06055.title: When Should Memory Stay Silent: Measuring Memory-Use Boundaries in Memory-Augmented Conversational Agents
2606.06055.paper_summary: Long-term memory enables language model agents to support personalized interactions, but it remains unclear when available memories warrant integration into responses. Existing memory evaluations emphasize retrieval accuracy and downstream task utility, while overlooking whether retrieved sensitive memory content is warranted in the current turn. We introduce RBI-Eval, a controlled measurement study built around a probe set that compares model behavior with and without access to sensitive memory under identical benign prompts. We evaluate four base LLMs against a matched no-memory reference across four memory-access settings: full-context exposure and three retrieval systems. Our results reveal substantial behavioral divergence. With memory available, the separation score for sensitive-memory integration decreases by 8.9\%--26.6\% relative to the matched no-memory reference for GPT-5.4-mini, but by 51.1\%--82.9\% for Claude-Sonnet-4.6, DeepSeek-V4-Flash, and Qwen3.5-9B. Control experiments on DeepSeek and GPT-5.4-mini show this effect is specific to sensitive content, rather than general personalization. Retrieval systems reduce exposure but do not eliminate integration once sensitive memory reaches the generator. These findings suggest safe personalization requires memory-aware decisions at both retrieval and generation time.
2606.06055.paper_summary_zh: 長期記憶使語言模型代理能夠支持個性化互動，但目前仍不清楚何時可用的記憶應該整合到回應中。現有的記憶評估強調檢索準確性和下游任務的效用，同時忽視了在當前回合中檢索到的敏感記憶內容是否合理。我們引入了RBI-Eval，一項圍繞探測集構建的控制測量研究，該研究比較了在相同的良性提示下，模型在有無訪問敏感記憶的情況下的行為。我們在四個記憶訪問設置中，對四個基礎LLM進行評估，這些設置包括完全上下文暴露和三個檢索系統。我們的結果顯示出顯著的行為差異。在有記憶可用的情況下，GPT-5.4-mini對於敏感記憶整合的分離分數相對於匹配的無記憶參考減少了8.9\%--26.6\%，但Claude-Sonnet-4.6、DeepSeek-V4-Flash和Qwen3.5-9B則減少了51.1\%--82.9\%。對DeepSeek和GPT-5.4-mini的控制實驗顯示，這一效應是針對敏感內容的，而非一般的個性化。檢索系統減少了暴露，但一旦敏感記憶到達生成器，則不會消除整合。這些發現表明，安全的個性化需要在檢索和生成時都進行記憶意識的決策。
2606.06055.author: Lingxiang Xu et.al.
2606.06055.authors: Lingxiang Xu, Jiaoyun Yang, Min Hu, Hongtu Chen, Ning An
